subject: Human Resources
title: Flexible Benefits Plans
description: A micro-lesson that makes Flexible Benefits Plans usable.

hook.text: A 25-year-old single employee and a 50-year-old with a family get the same benefits package. Neither is fully served.

buildup.text: Flexible benefits—or cafeteria plans—let employees choose from a menu of options based on their personal needs.

discovery.text: Employees allocate a benefits budget across health plans, retirement, childcare, education, wellness, and more.

twist.text: Flexibility increases satisfaction but adds administrative complexity. Companies need systems to manage diverse choices.

climax.text: Modern benefits platforms make customization easy—employees pick what matters to them from a digital marketplace.

punchline.text: One-size-fits-all benefits fit no one. Choice is the new standard.

quiz.question: What is the main advantage of flexible benefits plans?
options: Employees choose options that match their personal needs
They reduce all costs to zero
